Class EventPoster

java.lang.Object
com.example.codebase.EventPoster
public class EventPoster extends Object
Represents an event poster image stored in a Base64-encoded format.

This class handles the storage and decoding of event poster images. Images are encoded as Base64 strings to allow storage in Firestore, which does not natively support binary data.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    EventPoster()
    Default no-argument constructor required for Firestore deserialization.
    EventPoster(String posterImageBase64)
    Constructs an EventPoster with the specified Base64-encoded image string.

    • EventPoster

      public EventPoster()
      Default no-argument constructor required for Firestore deserialization.

    • EventPoster

      public EventPoster(String posterImageBase64)
      Constructs an EventPoster with the specified Base64-encoded image string.
      Parameters:
      posterImageBase64 - the Base64-encoded string representation of the poster image; must not be null or empty

  • Method Details

    • decodeImage

      public static Bitmap decodeImage(String base64Image)
      Decodes a Base64-encoded image string into an Android
      invalid reference 
      Bitmap
      .

      This static utility method converts a Base64 string back into a

      invalid reference 
      Bitmap
      object suitable for display in an ImageView or other UI components.

      Parameters:
      base64Image - the Base64-encoded string to decode; must not be null
      Returns:
      a
      invalid reference 
      Bitmap
      decoded from the provided Base64 string, or null if decoding fails

    • getPosterImageBase64

      public String getPosterImageBase64()
      Returns the Base64-encoded string representation of the poster image.
      Returns:
      the Base64-encoded poster image string, or null if not set

    • setPosterImageBase64

      public void setPosterImageBase64(String posterImageBase64)
      Sets the Base64-encoded string representation of the poster image.
      Parameters:
      posterImageBase64 - the Base64-encoded string to set; must not be null or empty
